title: Fissistigma affine
taxonomy: Fissistigma affine is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is native to China and is a member of the genus Fissistigma, which consists of various species of evergreen or semi-evergreen shrubs or small trees. The Annonaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as the custard apple family. Fissistigma affine is one of the many species within this family that are used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Further research may be needed to confirm the exact classification and relationships of Fissistigma affine within the Annonaceae family.
uses: Fissistigma affine is used in traditional medicine for its potential antimalarial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is also believed to have antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which could be beneficial in treating various health conditions.
